findings.title: Design and Implement Usage-Based Billing Engine
findings.description: Migrate from flat monthly pricing to a consumption-based pricing model where customers pay per metric ingested and per dashboard query. This requires a metering pipeline that tracks usage in real time, a rating engine to apply pricing rules, and integration with Stripe for invoicing.
assumptions: Customers will be billed accurately based on their usage.
The metering pipeline will function without significant downtime.

evidence.excerpt: Design and implement usage-based billing engine

recommendations: Ensure thorough testing of the billing engine before launch.

metadata.acceptance_criteria: GIVEN the new billing engine is implemented, WHEN a customer queries their usage, THEN the system should accurately reflect their usage and calculate the corresponding charges.

findings.title: Build Pricing Calculator and Cost Estimator
findings.description: Develop an in-app pricing calculator that estimates monthly costs based on current usage patterns, and a predictive cost dashboard showing projected spend for the next billing cycle.
assumptions: Customers will use the calculator to estimate costs.
The calculator will be accurate based on real-time data.

evidence.excerpt: Build pricing calculator and cost estimator for customers

recommendations: Conduct user testing to ensure the calculator is user-friendly.

metadata.acceptance_criteria: GIVEN a customer inputs their usage data, WHEN they use the pricing calculator, THEN the system should display an accurate estimated cost for the next billing cycle.

findings.title: Implement Usage Alerts and Spending Caps
findings.description: Create configurable spending alerts (50%, 75%, 90%, 100% of budget) and optional hard spending caps that pause non-critical data ingestion when exceeded, ensuring graceful degradation without data loss.
assumptions: Customers will configure their spending alerts.
The system will effectively pause data ingestion without loss.

evidence.excerpt: Implement usage alerts and spending caps

recommendations: Provide clear documentation on how to set up alerts.

metadata.acceptance_criteria: GIVEN a customer sets a spending cap, WHEN their usage exceeds this cap, THEN the system should pause non-critical data ingestion and notify the customer.

findings.title: Grandfather Existing Customers with 12-Month Price Lock
findings.description: Develop a grandfathering system that tracks customer cohorts, applies correct pricing tiers, and automatically transitions customers after the lock period with 60-day advance notice.
assumptions: Existing customers will be correctly identified and tracked.
The system will notify customers 60 days before their transition.

evidence.excerpt: Grandfather existing customers with 12-month price lock

recommendations: Ensure legal compliance in communication with customers.

metadata.acceptance_criteria: GIVEN a customer is grandfathered, WHEN the 12-month period is nearing its end, THEN the system should notify the customer 60 days in advance of their pricing change.
metadata.story_title: Implement Price Lock for Existing Customers

findings.title: Customer Churn Risk Analysis for Pricing Migration
findings.description: Create a model that identifies which customer segments will see cost increases vs. decreases under the new pricing, flagging accounts where projected spend increases >30% for proactive account management outreach.
assumptions: The model will accurately reflect customer usage patterns.
Account managers will act on flagged accounts.

evidence.excerpt: Customer churn risk analysis for pricing migration

recommendations: Regularly update the model based on customer feedback.

metadata.acceptance_criteria: GIVEN the churn risk model is implemented, WHEN a customer's projected spend increases by more than 30%, THEN the system should flag this account for outreach.

findings.title: Conduct Pricing Sensitivity Analysis Experiment
findings.description: Run an experiment to analyze customer sensitivity to pricing changes by offering different pricing tiers to select customer segments and measuring churn and engagement.
assumptions: Customers will respond to different pricing tiers.
The experiment will yield actionable insights.

evidence.excerpt: Conduct an experiment to analyze customer sensitivity to pricing changes

recommendations: Analyze results and adjust pricing strategy accordingly.

metadata.acceptance_criteria: GIVEN the pricing sensitivity analysis is conducted, WHEN customers are offered different pricing tiers, THEN the system should track churn and engagement metrics accurately.

findings.title: Implement Monitoring and Alerting for Billing System
findings.description: Set up monitoring and alerting for the billing system to ensure accurate tracking of usage and billing events, including error handling and reporting.
assumptions: Monitoring tools will be effective in detecting issues.
Alerts will be actionable for the team.

evidence.excerpt: Design and implement usage-based billing engine

recommendations: Regularly review monitoring logs for anomalies.

metadata.acceptance_criteria: GIVEN the monitoring system is in place, WHEN a billing error occurs, THEN the system should alert the team immediately.
metadata.story_title: Implement Monitoring and Alerting for Billing System

findings.title: Create Documentation for Pricing Changes
findings.description: Develop comprehensive documentation outlining the new pricing model, including FAQs, user guides, and support resources to assist customers during the transition.
assumptions: Customers will refer to documentation for guidance.
Documentation will be kept up-to-date.

evidence.excerpt: Design and implement usage-based billing engine

recommendations: Incorporate customer feedback into documentation updates.

metadata.acceptance_criteria: GIVEN the documentation is published, WHEN a customer accesses the pricing section, THEN they should find clear and helpful information regarding the new pricing model.

findings.title: User Feedback Collection Mechanism
findings.description: Implement a mechanism for collecting user feedback on the new pricing model, including surveys and feedback forms to gauge customer sentiment and areas for improvement.
assumptions: Customers will provide feedback on their experience.
Feedback will be actionable for product improvements.

evidence.excerpt: Conduct an experiment to analyze customer sensitivity to pricing changes

recommendations: Analyze feedback regularly to inform product decisions.

metadata.acceptance_criteria: GIVEN the feedback collection mechanism is implemented, WHEN customers provide feedback, THEN the system should record and categorize their responses accurately.

findings.title: Accessibility Compliance for Pricing Features
findings.description: Ensure that all new pricing features, including calculators and alerts, comply with accessibility standards to provide equal access to all users.
assumptions: Accessibility standards will be adhered to during development.
Users with disabilities will be able to use the features effectively.

evidence.excerpt: Build pricing calculator and cost estimator for customers

recommendations: Conduct accessibility testing with diverse user groups.

metadata.acceptance_criteria: GIVEN the pricing features are developed, WHEN tested for accessibility, THEN they should meet WCAG 2.1 AA standards.
metadata.story_title: Ensure Accessibility Compliance for Pricing Features

summary: The analysis has led to the creation of a comprehensive backlog of requirements focused on the transition to a usage-based pricing model. Key areas include the implementation of a billing engine, cost transparency features, spending alerts, and customer retention strategies. Each requirement is structured with clear acceptance criteria and dependencies, ensuring a well-defined path for development.
